behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is typically at the boundary between inventory availability data and ranking assembly (high-score item can leak through if filter/rank order is wrong). A pure unit test of one filter function can miss wiring/order bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with in-memory catalog + availability source + ranking module wired together. Include an out-of-stock item with highest relevance score and verify it is absent from final returned list.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between user settings retrieval and catalog filtering. Mocking either side can hide mapping/contract bugs (e.g., category IDs vs names).
behaviors.test_description: Wire settings store adapter and recommendation filter pipeline. Seed blocked categories in user settings and verify all products from those categories are excluded in output.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets ranking logic itself (weighting/scoring), not infrastructure boundaries. Deterministic scoring should be validated at function/object level.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the scoring/ranking function with fixed feature vectors: one product with strong recent-browsing match and one without. Assert score/order places browsing-match product higher.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Stable pagination failures usually come from interaction between sorting and pagination modules (especially tie-breaking), not a single function.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end recommendation assembly (rank + sort + paginate) on fixed dataset with ties. Request page 2 twice without data changes and assert identical item IDs and order.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic business rule in ranking/diversification logic, so minimum failure boundary is algorithm behavior, not external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test diversification/post-ranking function with user history covering categories A/B and candidate list containing A/B/C. Assert final top-N contains at least one item from unseen category C.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback depends on interaction between user-profile/history lookup and popularity source. Single-function tests can miss null/empty-handling at boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Wire profile/history provider + popularity provider + recommender. Use a user with no events and assert non-empty results are returned and match popularity ordering.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between UI/API click event payload, ranking context, and persistence. Mock-only unit tests can miss schema/field wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, simulate click on a specific position, and verify persisted tracking record contains exact product ID, shown position, and expected timestamp (using controlled clock).

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Performance SLO is sensitive to real runtime/OS/process behavior and full stack overhead. Lower-level tests with mocks can pass while production latency fails.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender in a real process with production-like configuration and a 100k-product dataset; issue real API calls and assert p95 latency is under 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: This depends on state transition across modules (recommendation exposure, purchase event ingestion/state update, next ranking). Boundary wiring is the core risk.
behaviors.test_description: Execute sequence: request recommendations, mark one recommended product as purchased, request again. With purchase-state store and ranking pipeline wired, assert purchased item rank drops below prior position (or is excluded per policy).

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism often breaks at module interactions (unstable iteration order, missing tie-breakers, data access ordering), not just inside one function.
behaviors.test_description: Run full recommendation pipeline twice for identical user state and identical catalog snapshot; assert exact same ordered product ID list across both runs.
